item.title: AI Post 作家 Fastessay.com 以及它如何扰乱作曲市场 | AI Post writer Fastessay.com and exactly how it Disrupts the Composing Market
item.description:
人工智能创建助手在工作中越来越受到人们的喜爱。 如果某些企业必须为任何不同的材料或令人着迷的领域制作内容文章，他们就会使用它们。 尽管数字计算机化公司利用这些技术为这里的消费者生成各种文章。 该企业自 2004 年成立以来，已帮助成千上万的人通过自己的论文任务获得 A 级。 他们可能已经收到了，任何人都可以使用全天候的周转时间以可承受的价格获取论文、考试文件、论文文件等等。 什么是 Fastessay.com？ 该组织十多年来一直致力于提供学术报告。 他们可能在临床调查、历史和数学等多元化教学专业工作领域有足够的经验。 该业务包括一个独立作家团队，他们绝对是多个地区的专业人士，并且还可以发布您想要的几乎任何文件。 此外，他们还提供指南和学术协助，以提供您在大学工作中可实现的最佳途径。 使用 Fastessay.com 完全从头开始编写文本消息 Fastessay.com 是希望完全从头开始提交文本信息的学生的理想选择。 这种帮助提供了许多不同的记录类型和问题，使您可以轻松获得最符合您偏好的记录。

当您需要从头写下文本内容时，有很多理由选择 Fastessay.com：

  • 定价对个人来说价格合理且便宜
  • 您会发现您的文件在三小时或更短的时间内送达
  • 帮助小组 24/7 全天候可用，因此获取或书面内容的任何困难都将立即得到解决
  • 该小组自 2004 年以来一直可用，因此他们知道他们可能面临的挑战！ 他们仅通过摆脱作家的禁令并在级别上生成信息提示来为该内容的自由撰稿人提供帮助。 人工智能创建助手在工作中越来越受到好评。 一些企业在必须为您的特定主题或关注区域撰写文章时会使用它们。 即使计算机化的公司利用它们来为他们的买家制作各种内容。 人工智能能够通过大量信息吸引大量受众。 这可能允许他们为利基市场提供帖子，否则这些市场将争夺合适的作家。 公司可以使用人工智能创建助手来迅速解决意料之外的或新的行业趋势，同时利用不断变化的电子数字数字印刷环境保持节奏。 究竟什么是 Fastessay.com 以及它的实际运作方式？ 它提供各种论文版本，包括论文、论文、演讲等，以及一些其他格式变化和引用类型，以满足客户的需求。 他们的创建者提供 24/7 全天候服务，通常非常有助于提供免费的修订，并在提供报告之前回答您可能对报告有疑问的问题。

item.title: 衡量团队成功 | Measuring team success
item.description:
您需要衡量团队的成功应该是一个必然的结论。 毕竟，我们得到了我们所测量的。 拥有成功的团队听起来是件好事。

虽然我们所说的成功团队是什么意思？ 我们的意思是高性能吗？ 我们的意思是快乐吗？ 我们不能通过一个指标来衡量一个团队来确定他们是否成功，这是一个 22 条规则，我们需要从团队成功的含义开始，然后我们可以创建与之匹配的指标。

你也许可以填补一些空白，但值得深入研究，什么是成功的团队？

  • 新功能可以快速交付
  • 生产中用户报告的错误数量很少
  • 团队成员有动力，甚至可能很开心
  • 当出现问题时，团队会加紧提供修复
  • 团队是他们领域的领导者，他们创新
  • 新团队成员快速有效地加入
  • 团队成员流失率低
  • 有其他东西，将其添加到列表中，重要的是通过查看结果清楚地说明您认为成功的团队意味着什么。 不要说诸如“优先考虑待办事项”、“有追溯”、“正确估计”或“是否进行冲刺”之类的傻话。 这些是活动，而不是关注结果=>“产生有意义的业务影响”、“团队士气随着时间的推移而提高”、“经常交付”或“在正确的时间应用正确的结构”。

    最差的指标

    既然我们对成功的团队是什么样子有了共同的理解，那么我们应该使用哪些指标来捕捉这一点？ 正确的指标应该只关注我们所说的内容，并明确排除任何不相关的内容。 关键错误将是团队无法控制结果的指标。 在这里，我将从永远不应该使用的指标开始，以及为什么。

    • 对象和关键结果（也称为 OKR）跟踪和关键结果完成
    • 冲刺目标完成
    • 速度 - 完成的票数或故事点数
    • 提交的代码行数

    为什么？

    我倾向于不使用 sprint 目标或 OKR。 虽然我认为 OKR 非常适合推动团队和业务之间的一致性、透明度和正确的优先级排序，但它们的“交付/状态/价值”与团队完全无法控制的两件事相关联：

    • 团队外的依赖

    我们实际上不希望 OKR 达到 100%，也不希望它们达到 10%。 如果我们永远无法达到 50%，那么我们就知道我们严重高估了自己的能力。 如果我们在季度结束前达到 100%，我们就低估了自己。 下一个 OKR 会为此进行调整，这意味着我们永远不会通过 OKR 指标取得成功。

    此外，OKR 存在于一个团队之外，它们涉及交付以及与其他团队和用户/客户的协作。 并且还根据产品愿景和业务战略的变化进行评估。 在任何情况下，您都无法通过团队无法控制的事情来衡量团队的成功。 这是不公平的，即使是完美的团队也无法承受由对指标的错误期望引起的问题。 证明这一点的一个极端例子是使 OKR 无效的关键支点，这意味着团队失败了，没有讨论的余地。

    此外，成功匹配目标的 KR，只告诉我们预测业务成功的能力，而不是实际衡量团队是否对此负责。 如果我们设定了一个目标并实现了它，那只意味着我们擅长设定目标，而不是实现目标。 如果您想管理一个不快乐的团队，请继续设定错误的目标。 团队与它无关。

    冲刺目标和速度是一样的。 它们非常适合对齐，但它们对于确定成功绝对是可怕的。 再一次，因为他们都在考虑事先估计你会做什么。 我们将我们的估计能力与我们对成功的定义结合起来。 一个能够有效地估计他们的工作的团队对于确定成功是没有用的。 而一个真正成功的团队是不需要估计的。 即使估计值是有价值的， 我们也会把它们弄错很多。 最重要的是，实现这些目标只是意味着我们估计得很好，而不是团队表现出色或成功。 如果我们想召集一个估算良好的团队——成功，请继续，但这是他们唯一擅长的事情，而且我们知道在软件中这没有什么价值。

    我希望我不需要进入代码行或任何与技术相关的东西，这些并不能告诉我们任何关于团队交付的价值，只是原始输出。 不要使用输出，使用结果。

    还有一个问题！

    估计不仅会影响使用 OKR 的能力，还有第二个问题。 由于您将基于此评估团队的成功和绩效，因此您的团队将在设计上干扰该过程。 您的团队已经在决定 OKR 将是什么，如果还要根据它们来评判他们，那么您已经为关键故障模式做好了准备。 强化系统设计问题是构建 OKR 以使团队看起来好或坏。 如果你可以通过一个过程故意让一个团队看起来好或坏，那么就有问题了，它应该尽可能客观。

    因此，一种常见的组织反模式是将被评判的团队排除在流程之外，并为他们分配 sprint 目标和 OKR。 这样做会使 OKR 变得毫无价值，因为重点是对齐； 分配他们会阻止团队加入。 此外，它会阻止他们成长到他们首先知道什么才是好的 OKR。

    这通常发生在对有效团队的样子缺乏了解的地方。 不是来自团队、由团队创建、由团队衡量和记录的 OKR 表明了有毒的领导。

    那么什么是正确的指标

    我们在上面列出的错误指标中发现了一个重要标准，那就是与估计和计划有关的任何事情。 我们永远不应该根据估计或遵循计划来衡量团队，而应该根据结果来衡量。 由于很难衡量“成功的客户”或“交付的价值”，因此我们需要代理。 一些公司喜欢使用“收入”，但这与可能不存在的业务和产品市场匹配度混为一谈，或者公司意外成功，就像大多数公司一样。

    当我很久以前开始从事工程设计时，对于什么是重要的并没有达成共识，我们可以谈论它，并且走得很远，但我们只是触及了它的边界。

    我们知道它必须做一些事情，比如交付速度、不浪费时间和“质量”。 我们还知道丰田方式的概念，这将有助于以一种好的方式将精益引入软件。 但谈论消除浪费与衡量成功是不同的。 当然，我们希望团队中的浪费为零，并且我们看到诸如交接之类的事情是错误的，因为它们会造成运输浪费。 或者做不直接交付给用户的工作是错误的，因为我们存货功能库存浪费。

    现在我们已经了解了衡量团队成功的因素和糟糕的因素，我们可以了解以下内容（也称为 DORA 指标，并在Accelerate一书中进行了深入讨论）：

    • 变更的准备时间——团队在知道他们想要做某事之后开始做某事需要多长时间。
    • 部署频率——团队部署到生产环境的频率、用户可见的更改、将这些更改拿出来，而不仅仅是合并。
    • 更改失败率——部署后出现新错误的频率。
    • 平均恢复时间——当出现错误时，需要多长时间才能恢复。

    基本上就是这样。 我们知道这些也是正确的指标，因为它们是可衡量的，它们并不代表带来另一个流程，这本身可能是有缺陷的。 除了我们没有精确测量的情况外，这里的估计为零。

    这并不是说你必须完美地衡量这些，只是你在衡量一个团队的成功时应该考虑这些。

    这是一个很好的开始，实际上我并没有止步于此，我还想为我的团队添加两个其他指标到此列表中，以防止其他标准团队出现故障。 一个常见的故障模式是当一个团队因为总是在正确的事情上工作而受到高度赞扬并且他们的所有客户都很高兴，但是每个与他们合作的团队都讨厌他们的工作方式（即他们工作的输出，而不是团队成员本身）。 同一组织中的其他团队甚至可能认为该团队做得很好，那是因为该团队将所有时间都花在了救火上。

    从长远来看，任何花费越来越多时间灭火的团队都会产生失控问题，最终会削弱公司。 如果必须花费越来越多的时间来解决已经创建的解决方案的问题，那么您将耗尽时间。 通常团队在没有时间的时候就已经意识到了这一点。 这就是为什么我关注的最重要的指标之一是：

    1. 用于灭火的时间百分比

    这也解决了上述指标具有巨大价值的问题，但您的组织认为团队从不致力于任何有价值的事情。 快速发布但设计流程不佳的团队往往在组织中具有高积极性，但通常最终成为关键瓶颈。 这是一个常见的问题模式。

    另一个角度是价值传递。 到目前为止，大多数指标都是面向内部的，有充分的理由，面向外部的指标被问题所污染，例如产品是否合适，我们是否拥有合适的用户，业务战略是否有意义？ 如果我们在其中一个方面遇到问题，我们不能让这些问题影响我们对团队成功的判断。 尽管如此，我觉得有必要制定一个衡量团队工作是否有价值的指标。 因为，如果一个团队没有完成有价值的工作，我们就会知道他们不会高兴。 所以，我的第二个奖励指标是：

    1. 支持/用户/客户请求的数量

    随之而来的是回答的准确性和时间，以及回答所需的人数。 我们希望在整个团队中实现水平分布、快速响应、低参与度。 任何团队成员都可以而且应该回答，而且他们应该是快速而明显的答案。

    我们可以将其用作驱动程序，以确保我们的文档（从面向外部的文档到内部文档和 api 规范）有意义且相关。 使用这个指标可以告诉我们我们正在构建什么，或者我们如何看待它是不正确的。 用户混淆是一个真正的问题，通常其根源是团队之外的人拥有产品，而不是团队拥有产品。 所有权可能看起来像一个错误地专注于功能而不是产品愿景的产品经理，或者一个专注于客户洞察力而不是捕捉业务潜在价值的业务分析师。 产品所有权在团队层面保持一致，没有其他地方。

    随着时间的推移跟踪这些是我知道团队在正确的领域花费他们的工作的方式。 还有一些运行时间较长的方面，例如很难与特定团队联系起来的工程保留，以及团队内团队成员的成长。 但是对于硬指标，我通常就到此为止了，但是这些其他方面都是很好的指标，所以我们也来谈谈它们。

    领先指标

    这总结了定量的客观指标。 然而，加入并引入这些可能是一个挑战，一个明显的后续是我们需要跟踪它们吗？ 答案几乎总是一个响亮的“否”。 谈论它们几乎总是足够的，而无需实际提出数字。 有不少 SaaS 产品试图为您跟踪它们，有些图表看起来不错，您可能会想设置它们并在外部共享它们以证明您的团队是成功的。 但是，成功地跟踪这些指标实际上本身就是一个指标，而不是上面列出的指标。 成为一个成功的团队与证明它有很大的不同，除非你想解决一个具体的问题，否则不必要地跟踪指标来证明团队是成功的，这是有毒领导的另一个指标。

    所以，如果我们实际上不跟踪这些，有什么好的指标可以知道正确的事情正在发生？ 你可以先用眼睛看，不难知道方向可以让事情变得更好，但也有明显的信号可以利用：

    工程师是否正在成长/被提拔（显然，出于正确的原因提拔的警告。）在这里，我使用基于谁参加/会议/分享/谈话/主持/领导的“直觉检查”。 如果始终是团队领导，那么我知道有问题。 如果其他人采取主动，这是一个好兆头。

    团队是否通过引入/测试新技术或领导/试验新产品理念进行创新。 这些都比较主观，但有感觉是判断团队成功与否的必要条件。 如果你不知道一个创新团队是什么样子，那么你可能不是判断一个团队是否成功的合适人选。

    空闲容量是在正确的时间烧掉技术债务以及处理“重要事情”的能力。 如果团队的进展速度比需要新功能的速度慢，那么他们的流程就不可扩展。 由于我希望软件团队能够自动化他们的任务，而不仅仅是交付，他们的责任就是让自己失业。 目标是随着时间的推移处理更重要的事情。

    如果他们没有能力扭转新特性或对它们进行优先级排序，那么我们要么做出了太大的改变并且不够敏捷，要么是我们之前的工作存在问题。 如果以前的工作妨碍了我们，它告诉我们团队没有以符合业务期望或产品构思/用户想法的方式解决问题。

    对于其他指标， Spotify 健康检查是一个很好的起点，但需要注意的是，显然您需要针对您想要培养的特定文化进行调整。

    包起来

    绝对不要使用他们自己在创建过程中存在缺陷的指标。 如果我们依赖于通过有缺陷的机制（例如估计）来衡量成功，那么我们会将团队的成功与一个毫无意义且通常是错误的指标结合起来。 你不会得到一个成功的团队，你不会真正知道你的团队是否成功，最糟糕的是，没有人会高兴。



原文:

It should be a inevitable conclusion that you to need to measure the success of your teams. After all, we get what we measure. And having successful teams sounds like a good thing.

Although what do we mean by a successful team? Do we mean high-performing? Do we mean happy? We can’t measure a team by a metric to find out of if they are successful, that’s a catch-22, we need to start with what it means that a team is successful, and then we can create metrics to match that.

You may be able to fill in the blanks a bit, but it’s worth diving into, what is a successful team?

  • New features can be delivered quickly
  • Low number of bugs reported from users in production
  • Team members are motivated and potentially even happy
  • When there is a problem the team steps up to deliver a fix
  • The team is a leader in their domain, they innovate
  • New team members onboard quickly and effectively
  • Low turn over rate for team members
  • [Bonus] They work on the right things at the right time

Have something else, add it to the list, what’s important is clearly state what you think a successful team means by looking at outcomes. Don’t say something dumb like “prioritizes backlog items”, “has retros”, “estimates correctly”, or “does sprints”. These are the activity, instead focus on outcomes => “delivers meaningful business impact”, “team morale improves over time”, “delivers frequently”, or “applies the right structure at the right time”.

The worst metrics

Now that we have a shared understanding of what a successful team looks like, what metrics should we use to capture this? The right metrics should be focused on only the things we said, and the explicitly exclude anything that isn’t related. The critical fault would be having a metric that the team can’t control the outcome. Here I’ll start with what metrics should never ever be used, and why.

  • Objects & Key Results (also known as OKRs) tracking and key results completion
  • Sprint goal completion
  • Velocity — Number tickets or story points completed
  • Lines of code code committed

Why?

I tend not use sprint goals nor OKRs. While I think OKRs are fantastic for driving alignment, transparency, and correct prioritization among teams and the business, their “delivery/status/value” is coupled to the two things that are completly out of the teams control:

  • The correctness of the OKRs
  • Dependencies outside the team

We actually don’t want OKRs to be at a 100% nor do we want them to be at 10%. OKRs success is our ability to drive motivation by trying to get to 100%. If we never get to 50% then we know we critical overestimate our abilities. If we get to a 100% before the quarter is over, we’ve underestimated ourselves. The next OKRs adjust for this, which means we will never be successful by an OKR metric.

Additionally OKRs exist outside of one team, they involve deliveries and collaboration with other teams and users/customers. And also are evaluated based on changes in Product visions and Business strategies. You cannot under any circumstance measure success of a team by something they can’t control. That’s just unfair, even the perfect team can’t sustain problems caused by faulty expectations on metrics. One extreme example that proves that point is a critical pivot that invalidates an OKR would means that team failed, without room for discussion.

Further, successfully matching the Objective’s KR, tells us only about the ability to predict the business success than actually measuring if the team is responsible for it. If we set a goal and we hit it, that only means that we are great at setting goals, not at hitting them. If you want to run an unhappy team, keep setting bad goals. The OKRs won’t be hit, and your outcome will be blaming the team. Team had nothing to do with it.

Sprint goals and velocity go the same way. They are great for alignment, but they are absolutely terrible for determining success. Again, because both of them look at estimating what you will do before hand. We couple our ability to estimate to our definition of success. A team being able to effectively estimate their work is useless for determining success. And a real successful team doesn’t need to estimate. Even if estimates had a value, we are going to get them wrong, by a lot. And most importantly meeting these goals just means we estimated well, not that the team is performing or successful. If we want to call a team that estimates well — successful, go ahead, but that’s the only thing they’ll be good at, and we know in the software this has very little value.

And I hope that I don’t need to get into Lines Of Code or anything that is hands on technical related, these don’t tell us anything about the value of team delivery, just the raw output. Don’t use output, use outcomes.

One more problem!

Not only does estimation contaminate the ability to use OKRs, there’s a second issue. Since you’ll be evaluating team success and performance based on this, your teams will by design interfere with the process. Your teams are already deciding what the OKRs will be, and if they are to be judged on them as well, then you’ve set yourself up for a critical failure mode. The reinforcing system design problem is then building OKRs to make the team look good or bad. If you can intentionally make a team look good or bad by a process then there is something wrong, it should be as objective as possible.

As a result, a common organizational antipattern is to take the team being judged out of the process and assign them sprint goals and OKRs. Doing so renders the OKRs worthless, since the whole point is alignment; assigning them prevents teams from being on-board. Further, itprevents them from growing to the point where they know what makes a good OKR in the first place.

Usually this happens in places where there is a lack of understanding of what effective teams look like. And OKRs that not sourced from the team, created by the team, measured and record by the team, is indicative of toxic leadership.

So what are the right metrics

We’ve uncovered an important criterion in the wrong metrics listed above, and that’s anything related to estimation and planning. We should never measure a team on estimation or following a plan, but instead on outcomes. Since it is really hard to measure “successful customers” or “value delivered” we need proxies for this. Some companies like to use “revenue”, but that’s conflated with the business and Product-Market-Fit which may be non-existent, or the company is accidentally successful, like most out there.

When I started engineering a long time ago, there was no consensus on what was important, we could talk about it, and get pretty far, but we were only touching on the boundaries of what that is.

We knew it had to do something like speed of delivery, not wasting time, and “quality”. We also knew concepts from the Toyota Way, would help introduce Lean to software in a good way. But talking about removing wastes is different from measuring success. Sure we want the wastes to be zero in the team, and we see how things like hand-offs are wrong, because they create transportation waste. Or doing work that isn’t delivery directly to users is wrong, because we stock on feature inventory waste.

Now that we have some understanding of what makes good for measuring team success and what’s atrocious we can land on the following (also known as DORA metrics, and heavily discussed in the book Accelerate):

  • Lead time for changes— How long it takes the team to start working on something after they know they want to work on it.
  • Deployment frequency — How often a team deploys to production, visible changes for users, getting those changes out, not just merging.
  • Change failure rate — How often after a deployment there is a new bug.
  • Mean time to recovery — When there is a bug how long does it take to recover.

And that’s basically it. We know these are also the right metrics, because they are measurable, and they don’t represent bringing another process, which in itself could be flawed. There is zero estimation here, other than the cases where we don’t have precise measurements for them.

And that isn’t to say that you have to perfectly measure these, just that you should be thinking about these when measuring the success of a team.

[Bonus] The advanced metrics

Those are a great start, and I actually don’t stop there, there are two other metrics I like to add to this list for my teams, which prevent other standard team disfunctions. One common disfunction pattern is when a team is highly praised for always working on the right thing and all their customers are happy, but every team that works with them hates the way they work (i.e. the output of their work, not the team members themselves). Other teams in the same org could even think that team is doing a good job, and that’s because the team is spending all their time fighting fires.

Long term any team that spends an increasing amount of time fighting fires is creating a run-away problem that will eventually cripple a company. If more and more time has to be spent on resolving issues with already created solutions, then you will run out of time. And usually teams realize this already when they have no time left. That’s why one of the most important metrics I focus on is:

  1. Percent time spent on fighting fires

This also solves the problem of having great values for the above metrics, but your organization thinks the team never works on anything valuable. Teams that are quick to release, but have a poor design process, tend to have high positive optics in an org but usually end up being a critical bottleneck. This is a frequent problem pattern.

The other angle from this is value delivery. So far most of the metrics are internal facing, with good reason, external facing metrics are contaminated with problems like is the product the right one , do we have the right users, does the business strategy make sense? If we have problems in one of these areas we can’t let those affect our judgment of the team’s success. Nonetheless, I feel the need to have a metric that tracks if the team’s work has value. Because, if a team isn’t completing valuable work, we’ll know they won’t be happy. So, my bonus second metric is:

  1. The number of support/user/customer requests

Along with this comes the accuracy and time to answer, along with the number of people required to answer. We want a level distribution across the whole team, quick responses, with low involvement. Any team member can and should answer, and they should be quick and obvious answers.

We can use this as a driver to make sure our documentation (from external facing docs, to internal ones, and api specifications) makes sense and is relevant. Using this metric tells us what we are building, or how we are thinking about it isn’t right. User Confusion is a real problem, and usually the source is that instead of the team owning the product, the product is owned by someone outside the team. Ownership may look like a Product Manager that is incorrectly focused on features rather than product vision, or a Business Analysts focused on customer insights rather than capturing the potential value for the business. Product ownership is aligned at the team level, no where else.

Tracking these over time are ways I know that the team is spending their work in the right area. There are some longer running aspects such as engineering retention which can be hard to link to a specific team, and also team member growth within the team. But for hard metrics, I usually stop here, but these other aspects are great indicators, so let’s talk about them as well.

Leading indicators

That wraps up the quantitative objective metrics. However, it can be a challenge to jump in and introduce these and an obvious followup is Do we need to track them? The answer is almost always, a resounding NO. It is almost always enough to talk about them, without actually putting up numbers. There is no small number of SaaS products attempting to track them for you, and some graphs look nice, and you may be tempted to set them up and share them externally to prove that your team is successful. However, doing the tracking these metrics successfully, is actually a metric in itself, and not one that is listed above. Being a successful team is very different from proving it, and unless there is a concrete problem you want to fix, unnecessarily tracking metrics to prove the team is successful is another indicator of toxic leadership.

So, if we don’t actually track these, what are some good indicators for knowing that the right things are happening? You can start by looking with your eyes, it isn’t hard to know the direction to make things better, but there are also obvious signals that can be utilized:

Are engineers growing/being promoted (obviously with the caveat of being promoted for the right reasons.) Here I use a “gut check” based on who is attending/meeting/sharing/talking/moderating/leading. If it is always the team lead, then I know there is a problem. If other people are taking initiative, that’s a good sign.

Are the teams innovating either from pulling in/testing new technology or leading/experimenting with new product ideas. These are more subjective, but having a feel is a requirement to being able to judge the success of a team. If you don’t know what a team innovating looks like, then you probably aren’t the right person to be judging if a team is successful.

Free capacity is the ability to burn down tech debt at the right time, as well as working on “important things”. If teams are moving slower than new features are needed, then their process isn’t scalable. Since I expect software teams to automate their tasks, not just deliver, their responsibility is to put themselves out of a job. With goal of working on more important things over time.

If they don’t have the ability to turn around new features or prioritize them, we are either making too big changes and not being agile, or have problems with our previous work. If previous work is getting in our way, it tells us the teams aren’t solving problems in a way that matches the expectations of the business or how the product is conceived/how our users think.

For additional indicators, the Spotify Health Check is a great place to start, with the caveat, obviously you need to adjust for the specific culture you want to cultivate.

Wrapping up

Absolutely don’t use metrics that they themselves are flawed in creation. If we depend on measuring success by a flawed mechanism (estimation for instance), then we couple the success of a team to a meaningless and often wrong metric. You won’t get a successful team, you won’t actually know if your team is successful, and worst of all worlds, no one will be happy.
item.author: wparad

item.title: 为您的技术职业制定路线图 | Creating a Roadmap for Your Tech Career
item.description:
许多人想过渡到科技行业，但不知道如何去做，不知道他们适合哪里，最适合他们的角色等等。

你不必那么担心，有很多问题和疑问是正常的，每个人都在这一点上，但你会弄清楚的。 技术领域有多种职业可供选择，从应用程序开发和 Web 开发等开发人员角色到产品设计和项目管理等非开发人员角色。 职业机会很多，在缩小选择范围时，您可能会发现自己处于十字路口。

虽然很难知道最适合您的技术职业道路是什么，但这里有一些有用的提示，可帮助您选择最适合您的技术职业道路（选自 Yewande Odumosu 的Designing a Tech Career Roadmap ；

一世。 评估你现在在哪里

了解您的身份和位置至关重要，尤其是在您的职业方面。 实际上，您以前的工作，去过的地方，家人和朋友都对您的决定很重要。
重新评估你已经走了多远，你在职业生涯中处于什么位置，并回答一些问题，例如“人们说我擅长什么？”、“是什么激励我？”、“我喜欢我的工作/课程的哪个方面最多还是最少？ 等等，将是帮助您找出最适合您的技术职业道路的好方法。

了解您的优势和技能

了解您现在拥有的技能、对您来说很容易掌握的技能、随着时间的推移而开发的技能以及您仍想学习的技能将帮助您决定最需要您的技能并与之相关的技术职业. 你已经获得的一些技能是可以转移的，可以引导你走向理想的技术职业。 相信我，无论您认为自己的技能多么微不足道，您都可以在科技领域提供一些东西。

三。 研究

在您反思并列出您的硬技能和软技能之后，就该进行研究了。 这将为您提供有关技术领域的各种职业以及最适合您的职业的线索（因为您知道您拥有该特定职业道路的可转移技能）。

除了您的研究之外，以下这些资源可以进一步指导您；

数据科学

产品设计

除了阅读上述资源外，还可以参加入门课程或速成课程（ youtube 、 classcentral 、 freecodecamp 、 udemy等上有很多资源）。 加入社区或志愿者担任您认为会为您的职业兴趣提供更多知识和经验的特定角色。 如果在工作中，您意识到自己的实力和技能更适合不同的技术角色，您可以自由切换到更适合的职业道路并且毫不犹豫地这样做，那么您的技术之旅绝不能是线性的.

紧张情绪来了

照片由 Adob​​e Stock上的EtiAmmos 拍摄

在完成所有研究并选择技术职业道路后，您必须了解可能会出现自我怀疑、紧张不安、恐惧、不确定性等。但是，我鼓励您超越自己的感受并保持专注. 坚持不懈地度过难关，如果你保持一致、坚定和好学，你会发现在接下来的几个月里，你会为自己取得的成就感到庆幸。

这是来自heelsandtech的一个测验，它将帮助您弄清楚您应该走哪条科技职业道路。

单击此链接以参加非凡的高跟鞋和科技职业测验。

你有这个！！！



原文:

Many people want to transition into tech but do not know how to go about it, where they fit in, the best role suitable for them, etc.

You do not need to be so worried, it is normal to have lots of questions and doubts, everyone does at this point but you will figure it out. There is a variety of careers available in the world of technology, ranging from developer roles like app development and web development to non-developer roles like product design and project management. The career opportunities are numerous and you may find yourself at a crossroad when it comes to narrowing down your choices.

While it can be difficult to know what the best technology career path is for you, here are a few helpful tips to enable you to choose the best tech career path for you (Culled from Designing a Tech Career Roadmap by Yewande Odumosu;

i. EVALUATING WHERE YOU ARE NOW

Understanding who and where you are is essential, especially in terms of your profession. In actuality, your previous jobs, where you've gone, family, and friends all matter to your decision.
Reassessing how far you have come, where you are in your career and answering a few questions like ” what do people say I am good at?”, “what motivates me?”, ”What aspect of my work/course do I enjoy the most or the least? and so on, would be good pointers to helping you figure out what tech career path might be best suitable for you.

ii. UNDERSTANDING YOUR STRENGTHS AND SKILLS

Being aware of the skills that you have right now, the ones that come easily to you, the ones you have developed over time and the ones you still want to learn will help you decide on what tech career your skills are most needed and relevant to. Some of your already acquired skills are transferable and can lead you to your ideal tech career. Trust me, no matter how insignificant you might think your skill is, you have something to offer in the tech space.

iii. RESEARCH

After you have reflected, and listed your hard and soft skills, it's time to do your research. This will give you a clue about the diverse careers in technology and the one(s) that are best suited for you (because you know that you have transferable skills for that particular career path).

In addition to your research, these resources below can further guide you;

Data Science

Web Development

Product Design

Difference Between A Web Developer And Web Designer

Apart from reading the resources above, take an intro class or crash course(there are lots of resources on youtube, classcentral, freecodecamp, udemy etc). Join a community or volunteer for a particular role you think will open you up to more knowledge and experience in your career interest. If while on the job, you realize that your strength and skills are more suitable for a different tech role, you are free to switch to a better-suited career path and do not hesitate to do so, your tech journey mustn't be linear.

Here Comes The Jitters

Photo by EtiAmmos on Adobe Stock

After you have done all your research and you have chosen a tech career path, you must understand that self-doubts, jitters, fear, uncertainties and the likes might set in. However, I encourage you to look beyond what you feel and remain focused. Persevere through the discomfort and if you stay consistent, determined and studious, you will find that in the next few months, you'll pat yourself on the back for how far you have come.

Here is a quiz from heelsandtech that will help you figure out what tech career path you should tow.

Click this link to take the phenomenal Heels and Tech Career Quiz.

You've got this!!!
item.author: vhinwe

item.title: 使用 Golang 和 Mongodb 的 Telegram 机器人 | Telegram bot with Golang and Mongodb
item.description:
马蒂亚斯·吉亚

发表于所以在周末我正在研究一个电报机器人，其任务是管理一个特定的电报组。 想知道机器人做什么？

  1. 该机器人欢迎新添加的用户加入电报组
  2. 删除管理员以外的用户发送到组的链接
  3. 删除新用户发送到群组的每条消息，直到他们将一定数量的用户添加到群组中，然后他们可以向群组发送消息

在本文中，我将向您展示如何使用 Golang 和 Mongodb 创建一个电报机器人。 首先在电报上搜索“botfather”并创建一个新机器人。 在您为机器人设置名称和用户名后，将向您发送一个令牌。 此令牌将用于验证您的机器人。

这是 .env 配置：

 TOKEN =<generated token from botfather in telegram> USER _NAME=<the group admin username> MONGO _URI=mongodb://localhost:27017<for local host mongodb> DATABASE _NAME=<database name>

这是机器人的代码：

 package tele import ( "encoding/json" "fmt" "log" "os" "strings" tgbotapi "github.com/go-telegram-bot-api/telegram-bot-api/v5" "github.com/hisyntax/telebot/database" "go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/bson" ) func urlChecker ( character string ) bool { var val bool //check if the string contains a . if strings . Contains ( character , "." ) { for i , value := range character { //get the index of . if string ( value ) == "." { fmt . Printf ( "%d - %v 
 " , i , string ( value )) prev := i - 1 next := i + 1 for e , v := range character { //check the previous character if its an "" string if e == prev { if string ( v ) != " " && string ( v ) != "." { //check the next character if its an "" string for ee , vv := range character { if ee == next { if string ( vv ) != " " && string ( vv ) != "." { val = true } } } } } } } } } return val } func Bot () { token := os . Getenv ( "TOKEN" ) bot , err := tgbotapi . NewBotAPI ( token ) if err != nil { log . Panic ( err ) } bot . Debug = true log . Printf ( "Authorized on account %s" , bot . Self . UserName ) u := tgbotapi . NewUpdate ( 0 ) u . Timeout = 60 updates := bot . GetUpdatesChan ( u ) for update := range updates { if update . Message != nil { log . Printf ( "[%s] %s 
 " , update . Message . From . UserName , update . Message . Text ) fmt . Printf ( "This is that message text gottent fron update: %s......... 
 " , update . Message . Text ) // THESE APPLIES TO EVERYONE IN THE GROUP EXCEPT THE ADMIN OF THE GROUP foundUser := update . Message . From . UserName if foundUser != os . Getenv ( "USER_NAME" ) { //welcome new users wc := update . Message . NewChatMembers if wc != nil { var byt [] map [ string ] string jsonM , _ := json . Marshal ( wc ) json . Unmarshal ( jsonM , & byt ) for val := range byt { if byt [ val ][ "first_name" ] != "" { firstname := byt [ val ][ "first_name" ] fmt . Printf ( "This is the firstname found %v..... 
 " , firstname ) sendMsg := fmt . Sprintf ( "Hi %s, Welcome to contact gain with EXODUS kindly add a minimum of 30 telegram user's to be able to drop your number and get verified into the VCF 🗃️ file, if you can't follow the process send a DM to @EXODUSTV to pay #500 and get verified to the VCF file" , firstname ) msg := tgbotapi . NewMessage ( update . Message . Chat . ID , sendMsg ) bot . Send ( msg ) } } } if update . Message . NewChatMembers != nil { up := update . Message var addedUsers database . AddedUsers jsonr , _ := json . Marshal ( up ) json . Unmarshal ( jsonr , & addedUsers ) insertID , _ := database . CreateMongoDoc ( database . UserCollection , addedUsers ) fmt . Printf ( "Mongodb data created with ID: %v 
 " , insertID ) } usersFirstname := update . Message . From . FirstName //delete messages that contains link sent by other users aside from the admin domain := urlChecker ( update . Message . Text ) if domain { fmt . Println ( "Message contains a link..." ) deleteMsg := tgbotapi . NewDeleteMessage ( update . Message . Chat . ID , update . Message . MessageID ) bot . Send ( deleteMsg ) fmt . Println ( "deleted message that contains link..." ) fmt . Println ( deleteMsg ) //notify they user that links can't be sent to the group sendMsg := fmt . Sprintf ( "%s The message you sent contains a link or you failed to add a space after the full stop(.). Links cannot be sent to this group" , usersFirstname ) msg := tgbotapi . NewMessage ( update . Message . Chat . ID , sendMsg ) bot . Send ( msg ) } else { //if the messages sent to the group is not a link //check if the text message sent is not empty if update . Message . Text != "" { //check if the user have already added _ number of users to the group countFilter := bson . M { "from.firstname" : update . Message . From . FirstName } addedUserCount := database . CountCollection ( database . UserCollection , countFilter ) fmt . Printf ( "This is the number of users you have added to the group %v 
 ...." , addedUserCount ) userNum := 5 if addedUserCount < userNum { // delete the messages sent to the group by the user who have not added the set numbers of users deleteMsg := tgbotapi . NewDeleteMessage ( update . Message . Chat . ID , update . Message . MessageID ) bot . Send ( deleteMsg ) fmt . Println ( deleteMsg ) // and if not delete their message and notify them to first add _ numbers of users before they can send in messages usersToAdd := userNum - addedUserCount sendMsg := fmt . Sprintf ( "%s you have only added %v user(s). You need to add %v more user(s) to be able to send messages to this group. If you can't follow the process send a DM to @EXODUSTV to pay #500 and get verified to the VCF file" , usersFirstname , addedUserCount , usersToAdd ) msg := tgbotapi . NewMessage ( update . Message . Chat . ID , sendMsg ) // msg.ReplyToMessageID = update.Message.MessageID bot . Send ( msg ) } } } } } } }

这是mongodb代码：

 package database import ( "context" "fmt" "log" "os" "time" "github.com/joho/godotenv" "go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/mongo" "go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/mongo/options" ) var ( UserCollection * mongo . Collection = OpenCollection ( Client , "users" ) ) type AddedUsers struct { From From `json:"from"` NewChatMembers [] NewChatMembers `json:"new_chat_members"` } type From struct { ID int64 `json:"id"` FirstName string `json:"first_name"` LastName string `json:"last_name"` Username string `json:"username"` } type NewChatMembers struct { ID int64 `json:"id"` FirstName string `json:"first_name"` LastName string `json:"last_name"` Username string `json:"username"` } func DbInstance () * mongo . Client { if err := godotenv . Load (); err != nil { log . Println ( "no env gotten" ) } MongoDb := os . Getenv ( "MONGO_URI" ) client , err := mongo . NewClient ( options . Client () . ApplyURI ( MongoDb )) if err != nil { log . Fatal ( err ) } ctx , cancel := context . WithTimeout ( context . Background (), 10 * time . Second ) defer cancel () err = client . Connect ( ctx ) if err != nil { log . Fatal ( err ) } fmt . Println ( "successfully connected to mongodb" ) return client } var Client * mongo . Client = DbInstance () func OpenCollection ( client * mongo . Client , collectionName string ) * mongo . Collection { if err := godotenv . Load (); err != nil { log . Println ( "no env gotten" ) } databaseName := os . Getenv ( "DATABASE_NAME" ) var collection * mongo . Collection = client . Database ( databaseName ) . Collection ( collectionName ) return collection } func GetMongoDoc ( colName * mongo . Collection , filter interface {}) ( * AddedUsers , error ) { ctx , cancel := context . WithTimeout ( context . Background (), 10 * time . Second ) defer cancel () var data AddedUsers if err := colName . FindOne ( ctx , filter ) . Decode ( & data ); err != nil { return nil , err } return & data , nil } func GetMongoDocs ( colName * mongo . Collection , filter interface {}, opts ...* options . FindOptions ) ( * [] AddedUsers , error ) { ctx , cancel := context . WithTimeout ( context . Background (), 10 * time . Second ) defer cancel () var data [] AddedUsers filterCusor , err := colName . Find ( ctx , filter , opts ... ) if err != nil { return nil , err } if err := filterCusor . All ( ctx , & data ); err != nil { return nil , err } return & data , nil } func CreateMongoDoc ( colName * mongo . Collection , data interface {}) ( * mongo . InsertOneResult , error ) { ctx , cancel := context . WithTimeout ( context . Background (), 10 * time . Second ) defer cancel () insertNum , insertErr := colName . InsertOne ( ctx , data ) if insertErr != nil { return nil , insertErr } return insertNum , nil } func CountCollection ( colName * mongo . Collection , filter interface {}) int { ctx , cancel := context . WithTimeout ( context . Background (), 10 * time . Second ) defer cancel () count , err := colName . CountDocuments ( ctx , filter ) if err != nil { return 0 } return int ( count ) }

这是我的 main.go 文件中的代码：

 package main import ( "log" "github.com/hisyntax/telebot/tele" "github.com/joho/godotenv" ) func main () { if err := godotenv . Load (); err != nil { log . Println ( ".env not found" ) } tele . Bot () }

完成构建机器人之后，您需要将其部署到服务器。 在你的机器人成功部署到服务器后，将你的机器人添加到你希望它为你管理的组中，然后让机器人成为组中的管理员。 你的机器人现在已经设置好了:)

我希望这可以帮助您构建自己的电报机器人。 您可以查看此 GitHub 存储库github.com/hisyntax/telebot以获取完整代码。

一旦暂停，hisyntax 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，hisyntax 将能够再次评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，所有按 hisyntax 发布的帖子都将被隐藏，并且只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 hisyntax 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 hisyntax 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 hisyntax 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Mathias Jiya

Posted on Sep 5

So over the weekend I was working on a telegram bot whose task is to manage a particular telegram group. Wondering what the bot does?

  1. The bot welcomes newly added users to the telegram group
  2. Deletes links sent to the group by the users aside from the Admin
  3. Deletes every messages sent to the group by new users until they have added a certain number of users to the group and after words they can send messages to the group

In this article, I would be showing you how to create a telegram bot with Golang and Mongodb. First search for "botfather" on telegram and create a newbot. After you have set a name for you bot and a username, a token would be sent to you. This token would be used to authenticate your bot.

This is the .env config:

TOKEN=<generated token from botfather in telegram> USER_NAME=<the group admin username> MONGO_URI=mongodb://localhost:27017<for local host mongodb>
DATABASE_NAME=<database name>

This is the code for the bot:

package tele import ( "encoding/json" "fmt" "log" "os" "strings" tgbotapi "github.com/go-telegram-bot-api/telegram-bot-api/v5" "github.com/hisyntax/telebot/database" "go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/bson"
) func urlChecker(character string) bool { var val bool //check if the string contains a . if strings.Contains(character, ".") { for i, value := range character { //get the index of . if string(value) == "." { fmt.Printf("%d - %v
", i, string(value)) prev := i - 1 next := i + 1 for e, v := range character { //check the previous character if its an "" string if e == prev { if string(v) != " " && string(v) != "." { //check the next character if its an "" string for ee, vv := range character { if ee == next { if string(vv) != " " && string(vv) != "." { val = true } } } } } } } } } return val
} func Bot() { token := os.Getenv("TOKEN") bot, err := tgbotapi.NewBotAPI(token) if err != nil { log.Panic(err) } bot.Debug = true log.Printf("Authorized on account %s", bot.Self.UserName) u := tgbotapi.NewUpdate(0) u.Timeout = 60 updates := bot.GetUpdatesChan(u) for update := range updates { if update.Message != nil { log.Printf("[%s] %s
", update.Message.From.UserName, update.Message.Text) fmt.Printf("This is that message text gottent fron update: %s.........
", update.Message.Text) // THESE APPLIES TO EVERYONE IN THE GROUP EXCEPT THE ADMIN OF THE GROUP foundUser := update.Message.From.UserName if foundUser != os.Getenv("USER_NAME") { //welcome new users wc := update.Message.NewChatMembers if wc != nil { var byt []map[string]string jsonM, _ := json.Marshal(wc) json.Unmarshal(jsonM, &byt) for val := range byt { if byt[val]["first_name"] != "" { firstname := byt[val]["first_name"] fmt.Printf("This is the firstname found %v.....
", firstname) sendMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Hi %s, Welcome to contact gain with EXODUS kindly add a minimum of 30 telegram user's to be able to drop your number and get verified into the VCF 🗃️ file, if you can't follow the process send a DM to @EXODUSTV to pay #500 and get verified to the VCF file", firstname) msg := tgbotapi.NewMessage(update.Message.Chat.ID, sendMsg) bot.Send(msg) } } } if update.Message.NewChatMembers != nil { up := update.Message var addedUsers database.AddedUsers jsonr, _ := json.Marshal(up) json.Unmarshal(jsonr, &addedUsers) insertID, _ := database.CreateMongoDoc(database.UserCollection, addedUsers) fmt.Printf("Mongodb data created with ID: %v
", insertID) } usersFirstname := update.Message.From.FirstName //delete messages that contains link sent by other users aside from the admin domain := urlChecker(update.Message.Text) if domain { fmt.Println("Message contains a link...") deleteMsg := tgbotapi.NewDeleteMessage(update.Message.Chat.ID, update.Message.MessageID) bot.Send(deleteMsg) fmt.Println("deleted message that contains link...") fmt.Println(deleteMsg) //notify they user that links can't be sent to the group sendMsg := fmt.Sprintf("%s The message you sent contains a link or you failed to add a space after the full stop(.). Links cannot be sent to this group", usersFirstname) msg := tgbotapi.NewMessage(update.Message.Chat.ID, sendMsg) bot.Send(msg) } else { //if the messages sent to the group is not a link //check if the text message sent is not empty if update.Message.Text != "" { //check if the user have already added _ number of users to the group countFilter := bson.M{"from.firstname": update.Message.From.FirstName} addedUserCount := database.CountCollection(database.UserCollection, countFilter) fmt.Printf("This is the number of users you have added to the group %v
....", addedUserCount) userNum := 5 if addedUserCount < userNum { // delete the messages sent to the group by the user who have not added the set numbers of users deleteMsg := tgbotapi.NewDeleteMessage(update.Message.Chat.ID, update.Message.MessageID) bot.Send(deleteMsg) fmt.Println(deleteMsg) // and if not delete their message and notify them to first add _ numbers of users before they can send in messages usersToAdd := userNum - addedUserCount sendMsg := fmt.Sprintf("%s you have only added %v user(s). You need to add %v more user(s) to be able to send messages to this group. If you can't follow the process send a DM to @EXODUSTV to pay #500 and get verified to the VCF file", usersFirstname, addedUserCount, usersToAdd) msg := tgbotapi.NewMessage(update.Message.Chat.ID, sendMsg) // msg.ReplyToMessageID = update.Message.MessageID bot.Send(msg) } } } } } }
}

This is the mongodb code:

package database import ( "context" "fmt" "log" "os" "time" "github.com/joho/godotenv" "go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/mongo" "go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/mongo/options"
) var ( UserCollection *mongo.Collection = OpenCollection(Client, "users")
) type AddedUsers struct { From From `json:"from"` NewChatMembers []NewChatMembers `json:"new_chat_members"`
} type From struct { ID int64 `json:"id"` FirstName string `json:"first_name"` LastName string `json:"last_name"` Username string `json:"username"`
} type NewChatMembers struct { ID int64 `json:"id"` FirstName string `json:"first_name"` LastName string `json:"last_name"` Username string `json:"username"`
} func DbInstance() *mongo.Client { if err := godotenv.Load(); err != nil { log.Println("no env gotten") } MongoDb := os.Getenv("MONGO_URI") client, err := mongo.NewClient(options.Client().ApplyURI(MongoDb)) if err != nil { log.Fatal(err) } ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second) defer cancel() err = client.Connect(ctx) if err != nil { log.Fatal(err) } fmt.Println("successfully connected to mongodb") return client
} var Client *mongo.Client = DbInstance() func OpenCollection(client *mongo.Client, collectionName string) *mongo.Collection { if err := godotenv.Load(); err != nil { log.Println("no env gotten") } databaseName := os.Getenv("DATABASE_NAME") var collection *mongo.Collection = client.Database(databaseName).Collection(collectionName) return collection
} func GetMongoDoc(colName *mongo.Collection, filter interface{}) (*AddedUsers, error) { ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second) defer cancel() var data AddedUsers if err := colName.FindOne(ctx, filter).Decode(&data); err != nil { return nil, err } return &data, nil
} func GetMongoDocs(colName *mongo.Collection, filter interface{}, opts ...*options.FindOptions) (*[]AddedUsers, error) { ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second) defer cancel() var data []AddedUsers filterCusor, err := colName.Find(ctx, filter, opts...) if err != nil { return nil, err } if err := filterCusor.All(ctx, &data); err != nil { return nil, err } return &data, nil
} func CreateMongoDoc(colName *mongo.Collection, data interface{}) (*mongo.InsertOneResult, error) { ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second) defer cancel() insertNum, insertErr := colName.InsertOne(ctx, data) if insertErr != nil { return nil, insertErr } return insertNum, nil
} func CountCollection(colName *mongo.Collection, filter interface{}) int { ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second) defer cancel() count, err := colName.CountDocuments(ctx, filter) if err != nil { return 0 } return int(count)
}

This is the code in my main.go file:

package main import ( "log" "github.com/hisyntax/telebot/tele" "github.com/joho/godotenv"
) func main() { if err := godotenv.Load(); err != nil { log.Println(".env not found") } tele.Bot()
}

After which you are done building your bot, you would need to deploy it to a server. Heroku doesn't work well with it so I would highly recommend you deploy your bot on https://railway.app. After your bot have been successfully deployed to the server, add your bot to the group you want it to manage for you and then make the bot an admin in the group. Your bot is now set :)

I hope this helps you build your own telegram bot. You can checkout this GitHub repository github.com/hisyntax/telebot for the complete code.

Once suspended, hisyntax will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, hisyntax will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by hisyntax will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If hisyntax is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag hisyntax:

hisyntax consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging hisyntax will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: hisyntax

item.title: 如何构建 WEB API ASP.NET Core 6 | How to Build a WEB API ASP.NET Core 6
item.description:
安德烈斯·迪亚兹

发表于介绍

本教程是循序渐进系列的一部分：如何从 ASP.NET Core 的基础知识构建一个干净的 Web API。

我们开始使用 Net Core 6 构建客户 Web API
使用此框架上提供的本机依赖注入并利用实体框架和最常用的 nuget 包之一创建存储库模式， automapper遵循 Solid 原则。

如果您不了解 SOLID 原则，则可能需要在网络上进行研究。

我为什么要使用这个？

依赖注入

简而言之， 依赖注入是一种对象接收其他对象的设计模式。 一种控制反转的形式，依赖注入旨在分离构造对象和使用它们的关注点，从而导致松散耦合的程序。

您解决的一些问题是：

  • 使用接口或基类来抽象依赖实现。
  • 在服务容器中注册依赖项。 将服务注入到需要的类的构造函数中。 该框架负责创建依赖项的实例并在不再需要时将其处理掉。

在依赖注入中查看更多细节

存储库模式

根据 Martin Flower 的说法，存储库在域和数据映射层之间进行调解，就像内存中的域对象集合一样。 客户端对象以声明方式构造查询规范并将它们提交到存储库以获得满意。

在存储库模式中查看更多详细信息。

自动映射器

它是一个基于约定的对象到对象映射器，需要最少的配置。

一种常见的用途是当我们必须将一种对象类型映射到另一种时。 每次我们必须做的时候都做它很无聊，所以这个简单的工具为我们做的。

在自动映射器中查看更多详细信息

我们在 Customer API 项目中定义这些HTTP 方法，遵循标准约定来创建 restful 服务。

先决条件

  1. 在本例中，我们使用的是 Visual Studio 2022，在此处下载

  2. 创建一个 Web API 项目

    我们开始创建一个新项目

    步骤1

    从“文件”菜单中，选择“新建”>“项目”。 在搜索框中输入 Web API。 选择 ASP.NET Core Web API 模板并选择下一步。 在 Configure your new project 对话框中，将项目命名为 CustomerAPI 并选择 Next。 在附加信息对话框中：确认框架为 .NET 6.0（长期支持）。 确认选中使用控制器的复选框（取消选中以使用最少的 API）。

    选择创建。

第2步

第 3 步

您的解决方案应如下所示：

我们删除了所有不必要的文件 WeatherForecastController.cs 和 WeatherForecast.cs。

在这种情况下，我们选择 API 下的第一个选项 API Controller-Empty。

然后，我们创建控制器类CustomersController，继承自ControllerBase，在继续定义端点之前，开始定义必要的DTO。

如果您不熟悉 Dto 类，我们建议您阅读创建数据传输对象 (DTO) | 但总而言之，我要做的是创建一个 DTO，因为我们不想显示由与数据库相关的内部对象指定的所有列。 我们在 DTO 中仅定义我想在端点上公开的属性。

此时，我们要定义一个属性来重用相似的属性。 为此，我们只有一个属性，但在实际场景中，我们可能需要指定多个。

 namespace WebApiCustomers.Dtos ; public class BaseDto { public int Id { get ; set ; } }

以下 Dto 类是 CustomerReadDto、CustomerCreateDto 和 CustomerUpdateDto； 我们必须根据要求创建Dto。

我们有这些案例：

现有数据库中的脚手架模型类

因为在实际场景中，您可能会使用现有数据库。 在这种情况下，我们将在 Visual Studio 的 SQL Server 对象资源管理器选项卡上创建一个小数据库“CustomerDemoDb”。

第一步：通过右键单击 > New Database > CustomerDemoDb 创建一个 CustomerDemoDb 数据库，然后使用 New Query 并复制粘贴此语法。

 Use CustomerDemoDb CREATE TABLE [ dbo ].[ Customer ] ( [ Id ] INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY IDENTITY ( 1 , 1 ), [ FirstName ] NVARCHAR ( 150 ) NULL , [ LastName ] NVARCHAR ( 150 ) NULL , [ EmailAddress ] NVARCHAR ( 150 ) NULL , [ DateOfBirth ] DATE NULL , [ CreatedAt ] DATE NULL );

伟大的！ 我们创建了数据库，现在我们必须构建 DatabaseDbContext 以从该数据库中添加和获取记录。

为了创建数据库上下文，我们必须安装一些包：

要完成此操作，请转到 > Nuget Package Manager > Browse 键入它们中的每一个。

最后一个是执行我们在下一步中使用的命令“Scaffold-DbContext”所必需的。

在您的项目中创建数据文件夹，并在同一屏幕上，转到显示在底部的包管理控制台，然后键入以下命令：

请记住将 [ServerName] 替换为您的 Current ServerName。

 Scaffold-DbContext "Server=[ServerName]; Database = CustomerDemoDb ; Trusted_Connection = True ; " Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer -outPutDir Data

在这种方法中，我们使用“第一个数据库”创建我们的 DbContext。

您的数据文件夹应如下所示。

转到 CustomerDemoDbContext，复制 ConnectionString 并在 appsettings.json 上定义您的连接字符串。

 "ConnectionStrings" : { "CustomerDbConext" : "Server=[ServerName];Database=CustomerDemoDb;Trusted_Connection=True;" }

从 CustomerDemoDbContext 类中，删除 OnConfiguring 方法内的块，因为下一步在 NetCore 5 上的服务文件上的 File Program.cs 上定义我们的连接字符串是 Startup.cs。

在这行代码之后添加此代码：

var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);

要添加的代码：

 builder . Services . AddDbContext < CustomerDemoDbContext >( options => options . UseSqlServer ( builder . Configuration . GetConnectionString ( "CustomerDbConext" )));

这里我们使用依赖注入来注入 DbContext 服务。 恢复，我们可以通过 3 种方式注册服务：

如果您想了解更多关于 ASP.NET Core 中的依赖注入生命周期的详细信息，我建议您阅读这篇文章。

使用存储库模式和实体框架为 CRUD 操作创建通用存储库

在这个主题上，我们必须基于名为 Repository Pattern 的设计模式创建一个 Repository 类，其中一个常见用途是定义访问数据库记录的所有相关方法。

第一步是创建 Repositories 文件夹并定义 IRepository 接口，如下所示：

 namespace WebApiCustomers.Repositories ; public interface IRepository < TEntity > { Task < IEnumerable < TEntity >> GetAllAsync (); Task < TEntity ?> GetAsync ( int ? id ); Task AddAsync ( TEntity entity ); Task UpdateAsync ( TEntity entity ); Task DeleteAsync ( int id ); Task SaveAsync (); }

在这种情况下，我们利用 DbContext 中的通用类 TEntity 和 DbSet 对象，在下一步中进行说明。

在这里，我们使用 DbSet 以这种方式使用与数据库上下文相关的任何类来创建 Base Repository ：

 using Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore ; using WebApiCustomers.Data ; namespace WebApiCustomers.Repositories ; public class BaseRepository < TEntity > : IRepository < TEntity > where TEntity : class { private readonly CustomerDemoDbContext _context ; private readonly DbSet < TEntity > _dbset ; public BaseRepository ( CustomerDemoDbContext context ) { _context = context ; _dbset = _context . Set < TEntity >(); } public async Task AddAsync ( TEntity entity ) => await _dbset . AddAsync ( entity ); public async Task < IEnumerable < TEntity >> GetAllAsync () => await _dbset . ToListAsync (); public async Task < TEntity ?> GetAsync ( int ? id ) => await _dbset . FindAsync ( id ); public async Task DeleteAsync ( int id ) { var dataToDelete = await _dbset . FindAsync ( id ); _dbset . Remove ( dataToDelete ); } public async Task UpdateAsync ( TEntity entity ) { await Task . Run (()=> _dbset . Attach ( entity )); _context . Entry ( entity ). State = EntityState . Modified ; } public async Task SaveAsync () { await _context . SaveChangesAsync (); } }

现在我们必须创建 ICustomerRepository，因为有时您必须添加其他方法，并且您可以在此接口上定义它们。

 public interface ICustomerRepository : IRepository < Customer > { //Define your Additional Signature methods here }
 using Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore ; using WebApiCustomers.Data ; namespace WebApiCustomers.Repositories ; public class CustomerRepository : BaseRepository < Customer >, ICustomerRepository { private readonly CustomerDemoDbContext _context ; public CustomerRepository ( CustomerDemoDbContext context ) : base ( context ) => _context = context ; }

这个存储库模式通过一个表完成了它们的功能，但在实际场景中，您可能必须使用更多实体，现在我们需要进入程序并在本例中为 CustomerRepository 注入我们的存储库。

为此，我们必须在该行之后添加此代码

builder.Services.AddControllers();

对于通用存储库

builder.Services.AddTransient(typeof(IRepository<>),typeof(BaseRepository<>));

对于客户存储库

builder.Services.AddTransient<ICustomerRepository, CustomerRepository>();

使用 AutoMapper Nuget 包创建 Dto 和使用模型映射 Dto。

为什么使用 Dtos？，使用 Dtos 的目的是使用类来仅公开您需要的字段，公开我们表中的所有列并不比我们创建 I、类、仅需要的列更安全。

首先安装两个 AutoMapper Nuget 包，用于将我们的 Dto 映射到实体

然后需要在 un program.cs 文件中添加这一行

builder.Services.AddAutoMapper(Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly());

在此之后，我们必须定义配置文件类，我可以在其中定义我们需要的所有映射

然后我们可以开始为我们需要的所有必要的依赖注入添加我们的配置文件类，就像这样。

 using AutoMapper ; using WebApiCustomers.Data ; using WebApiCustomers.Dtos ; namespace WebApiCustomers.Profiles { public class CustomerProfile : Profile { public CustomerProfile () { CreateMap < Customer , CustomerReadDto >(); CreateMap < CustomerCreateDto , Customer >(). ForMember ( m => m . CreatedAt , o => o . MapFrom ( s => DateTime . Now . Date )); } } }

在第二行，CreateMap 为 CreatedAt 列指定了一个默认值，因为我们从系统而不是用户获取日期。

现在我们回到控制器并注入两个对象 ICustomerRepository 和 IMapper，你的构造函数类应该是这样的：

 private readonly ICustomerRepository _customerRepository ; private readonly IMapper _mapper ; public CustomersController ( ICustomerRepository customerRepository , IMapper mapper ) { _customerRepository = customerRepository ; _mapper = mapper ; }

为 CRUD 操作创建 HTTP 方法

现在需要定义上表中定义的每个 HTTP 方法

获取 /api/客户

在这里，我们要为 get 方法 /api/customers 定义操作，我们必须像这样返回客户数组：

 [ HttpGet ] public async Task < IActionResult > GetAll () { var listCustomers = await _customerRepository . GetAllAsync (); var result = _mapper . Map < List < CustomerReadDto >>( listCustomers ); return Ok ( result ); }

我们有三行代码：

客户名单：

 var listCustomers = await _customerRepository . GetAllAsync ();

将客户列表映射到 CustomerReadDto ：

 var result = _mapper . Map < List < CustomerReadDto >>( listCustomers );

返回响应：

获取 /api/customers/{id}

这里我们要定义一个get方法的动作/api/customers/{id}，我们必须返回一个CustomerReadDto对象，这里我们不必解释以下方法，因为太相似了：

 [ HttpGet ] public async Task < IActionResult > Get ( int id ) { var customerItem = await _customerRepository . GetAsync ( id ); var result = _mapper . Map < CustomerReadDto >( customerItem ); return Ok ( result ); }

发布 /api/客户

在这里，我们要为这样的 POST 方法 /api/customers 定义动作；

 [ HttpPost ] public async Task < IActionResult > PostCustomer ([ FromBody ] CustomerCreateDto customerCreateDto ) { var customerToInsert = _mapper . Map < Customer >( customerCreateDto ); await _customerRepository . AddAsync ( customerToInsert ); await _customerRepository . SaveAsync (); return CreatedAtAction ( "Get" , new { id = customerToInsert . Id }, customerToInsert ); }

放置 /api/customers/{id}

在这里，我们将像这样定义 PUT 方法 /api/customers/{id} 的操作；

 [ HttpPut ( "{id}" )] public async Task < IActionResult > UpdateCustomer ( int id , [ FromBody ] CustomerUpdateDto customerUpdateDto ) { if ( id != customerUpdateDto . Id ) return BadRequest (); var customerToUpdate = _mapper . Map < Customer >.( customerUpdateDto ); await _customerRepository . UpdateAsync ( customerToUpdate ); await _customerRepository . SaveAsync (); return NoContent (); }

删除 /api/customers/{id}

在这里，我们将像这样定义 Delete 方法 /api/customers/{id} 的操作；

 [ HttpDelete ( "{id}" )] public async Task < IActionResult > DeleteCustomer ( int id ) { await _customerRepository . DeleteAsync ( id ); await _customerRepository . SaveAsync (); return NoContent (); }

昂首阔步

该工具支持整个 API 生命周期的开发，从设计和文档到测试和部署。

如果您按照所有步骤定义项目，则可以使用 swagger 页面测试所有 HTTP 方法：

结论

我们了解了这些主题。

  • 依赖注入
  • 存储库模式
  • 自动映射器
  • 创建一个 Web API 项目
  • 为 CRUD 操作创建通用存储库

源代码 WebApiCustomers

如果你喜欢这篇文章，请订阅并关注这个关于构建干净的 web API Net Core 6 的系列。

一旦暂停，learnwithandres 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，learnwithandres 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，learnwithandres 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 learnwithandres 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 learnwithandres 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 learnwithandres 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Andrés Díaz

Posted on Sep 5

Introduction

This tutorial is Part of the Step by Step Series: How to build a Clean Web API from the basics of ASP.NET Core.

We are starting building a Customer Web API with Net Core 6
using the native dependency injection provided on this framework and creating a repository pattern taking advantage of the entity framework and one of the most used nuget packages, automapper following the Solid Principles.

You may need to research this on the web if you don't know the SOLID principles.

Why should I use this?

Dependency Injection

In short, dependency injection is a design pattern in which an object receives other objects. A form of inversion of control, dependency injection aims to separate the concerns of constructing objects and using them, leading to loosely coupled programs.

Some of the problems that you resolve are these:

  • Use of an interface or base class to abstract the dependency implementation.
  • Register the dependency in a service container. ASP.NET Core provides a built-in service container.
  • Inject the service into the class's constructor where it needs. The framework takes on the responsibility of creating an instance of the dependency and disposing of it when it's no longer needed.

See more details in Dependency Injection

Repository pattern

According to Martin Flower A repository mediates between the domain and data mapping layers, acting like an in-memory domain object collection. Client objects construct query specifications declaratively and submit them to the repository for satisfaction. 

See more details in repository pattern.

AutoMappter

Automapper is a simple library that helps us to transform one object type into another. It is a convention-based object-to-object mapper that requires minimal configuration.

What problems will resolve automapper?

One of the common uses is when we have to map one object type to another. It is boring to do it every time we have to do it, so this simple tool makes it for us.

See more details in automapper

HTTP methods

We are defining these HTTP methods in our Customer API project following the standard conventions to create restful services.

Prerequisites

  1. In this case, we are using Visual Studio 2022, Download here

  2. SQL Express Edition or Developer Edition, Download here

Create a web API project

We start Creating a new project

Step 1

From the File menu, select New > Project. Enter Web API in the search box. Select the ASP.NET Core Web API template and select Next. In the Configure your new project dialog, name the project CustomerAPI and select Next. In the Additional information dialog: Confirm the Framework is .NET 6.0 (Long-term support). Confirm the checkbox for Use controllers(uncheck to use minimal APIs) is checked.

Select Create.

Step 2

Step 3

Your solution should look like this:

We remove all unnecessary files WeatherForecastController.cs and WeatherForecast.cs.

In this case, we choose the first option under API called API Controller-Empty.

And then, we create the controller class, CustomersController, inheriting from ControllerBase, and before continuing to define the endpoint, start defining the necessary DTO.

If you are unfamiliar with the Dto class, we recommend reading Create Data Transfer Objects (DTOs) | Microsoft Docs..

But to summarize, what I have to do is create a DTO because we don't want to reveal all our columns specified by internal objects related to our database. We define in our DTO only the properties that I want to expose on the endpoints.

At this point, we are to define one property to reuse similar properties. For this purpose, we only have one property, but in real scenarios, we may need to specify more than one.

namespace WebApiCustomers.Dtos;
public class BaseDto
{ public int Id { get; set; }
}

The following Dto Classes are CustomerReadDto, CustomerCreateDto, and CustomerUpdateDto; we must create Dto according to requirements.

We have these cases:

CustomerReadDto, used to read Customer Info and return from the Get Methods.
CustomerCreateDto, used in POST Method, for creating customer records.
CustomerUpdateDto, used in Put Method, for updating customer records.

namespace WebApiCustomers.Dtos;
public class CustomerReadDto
{ public string? FirstName { get; set; } public string? LastName { get; set; } public DateTime DateOfBirth { get; set; } public string? EmailAddress { get; set; }
}

namespace WebApiCustomers.Dtos;
public class CustomerCreateDto
{ public string? FirstName { get; set; } public string? LastName { get; set; } public DateTime DateOfBirth { get; set; } public string? EmailAddress { get; set; }
}

namespace WebApiCustomers.Dtos;
public class CustomerUpdateDto: BaseDto
{ public string? FirstName { get; set; } public string? LastName { get; set; } public DateTime DateOfBirth { get; set; } public string? EmailAddress { get; set; }
}

Scaffold Models Class from Existing Database

Because in real scenarios, you will likely work with an existing database. In this case, we are creating a little database "CustomerDemoDb" on SQL Server Object Explorer Tab From Visual Studio.

Step One: Create a CustomerDemoDb database by right-clicking > New Database > CustomerDemoDb and then using New Query and copy-pasting this syntax.

Use CustomerDemoDb CREATE TABLE [dbo].[Customer]
( [Id] INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY IDENTITY (1, 1), [FirstName] NVARCHAR (150) NULL, [LastName] NVARCHAR (150) NULL, [EmailAddress] NVARCHAR (150) NULL, [DateOfBirth] DATE NULL, [CreatedAt] DATE NULL
);

Great! We created the database, and now we have to build our DatabaseDbContext for adding and getting records from this database.

For creating the Database Context, we have to install some Packages:

Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer

Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Tools

To Accomplish This, Go to > Nuget Package Manager > Browse Type each of them.

The Last One is necessary to execute the command "Scaffold-DbContext" we are using in the next step.

Create Data Folder in your project, and on the same Screen, Go to Package Manage Console which shows it up at the Bottom, and Type this command :

Remember to replace [ServerName] with your Current ServerName.

Scaffold-DbContext "Server=[ServerName];Database=CustomerDemoDb;Trusted_Connection=True;" Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer -outPutDir Data

On This approach, we create our DbContext using "First Database."

Your Data Folder should look like this.

Go to CustomerDemoDbContext, Copy the ConnectionString and define your connecting string on appsettings.json.

"ConnectionStrings": { "CustomerDbConext": "Server=[ServerName];Database=CustomerDemoDb;Trusted_Connection=True;" }

From CustomerDemoDbContext Class, Remove the block inside the OnConfiguring Method because on the next step with defining our connectionstring on File Program.cs on services File on NetCore 5 is Startup.cs.

Add this code after this line of code:

var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);

Code to add :

builder.Services.AddDbContext<CustomerDemoDbContext>(options => options.UseSqlServer(builder.Configuration.GetConnectionString("CustomerDbConext")));

Here we are using Dependency Injection for injecting DbContext Service. The Method AddDbContext internally defines the LifeTime of Service AddScope for Injecting our DbContext.

Resuming, we can register services in 3 ways :

Transient, this is created each time on every request.
Scoped, this is created once and reused on the same request.
Singleton, this is created once and is reused across the application lifetime.

If you want more detail about Dependency Injection LifeTime in ASP.NET Core, I recommend reading this article.

Dependency Injection Lifetimes in ASP.NET Core — Code Maze (code-maze.com)

Create Generic Repository for CRUD Operations with Repository Pattern and Entity Framework

On this Topic, We have to create a Repository Class Based on a Design Pattern named Repository Pattern, and one of the common uses is to define all related methods for accessing database records.

The first step is to create Repositories Folder and define IRepository Interface like this:

namespace WebApiCustomers.Repositories;
public interface IRepository<TEntity>
{ Task<IEnumerable<TEntity>> GetAllAsync(); Task<TEntity?> GetAsync(int? id); Task AddAsync(TEntity entity); Task UpdateAsync(TEntity entity); Task DeleteAsync(int id); Task SaveAsync();
}

In this case, we take advantage of Generic Class TEntity and DbSet Object from DbContext, explained in the next step.

Here we are using DbSet to use any class related to the database context in this way to create Base Repository :

using Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore;
using WebApiCustomers.Data;
namespace WebApiCustomers.Repositories;
public class BaseRepository<TEntity> : IRepository<TEntity> where TEntity : class
{ private readonly CustomerDemoDbContext _context; private readonly DbSet<TEntity> _dbset; public BaseRepository(CustomerDemoDbContext context) { _context = context; _dbset = _context.Set<TEntity>(); } public async Task AddAsync(TEntity entity) => await _dbset.AddAsync(entity); public async Task<IEnumerable<TEntity>> GetAllAsync() => await _dbset.ToListAsync(); public async Task<TEntity?> GetAsync(int? id) => await _dbset.FindAsync(id); public async Task DeleteAsync(int id) { var dataToDelete = await _dbset.FindAsync(id); _dbset.Remove(dataToDelete); } public async Task UpdateAsync(TEntity entity) { await Task.Run(()=> _dbset.Attach(entity)); _context.Entry(entity).State = EntityState.Modified; } public async Task SaveAsync() { await _context.SaveChangesAsync(); }
}

Now we have to create the ICustomerRepository because sometimes you have to add additional methods, and you can define them on this interface.

public interface ICustomerRepository: IRepository<Customer>
{
//Define your Additional Signature methods here
}

using Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore;
using WebApiCustomers.Data; namespace WebApiCustomers.Repositories; public class CustomerRepository : BaseRepository<Customer>, ICustomerRepository
{ private readonly CustomerDemoDbContext _context; public CustomerRepository(CustomerDemoDbContext context) : base(context) => _context = context;
}

This Repository Pattern complete their function with one table but in real scenarios maybe you have to work with more entities, now we need to go, to the program and inject our repository in this case CustomerRepository.

To accomplish that, we have to add this code, after the line

builder.Services.AddControllers();

For the Generic Repository

builder.Services.AddTransient(typeof(IRepository<>),typeof(BaseRepository<>));

For the Customer Repository

builder.Services.AddTransient<ICustomerRepository, CustomerRepository>();

Creating Dto and Mapping Dto With Models using AutoMapper Nuget Package.

Why Use Dtos ?, the purpose of using Dtos is to use classes to expose only the fields you need, it is not safer to expose all columns from our table than we created I, classes, with only needed columns.

Firstly install two AutoMapper Nuget Package for Map ours Dto to Entities

· AutoMapper

· AutoMapper.Extensions.Microsoft.DependencyInjection

And then with need to add this line on un program.cs file

builder.Services.AddAutoMapper(Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly());

After this, we must define the profile class, where I can define all mapping that we need it

And then we can start to Add our profile class for all necessary Dependency Injection that we need, like this.

using AutoMapper;
using WebApiCustomers.Data;
using WebApiCustomers.Dtos;
namespace WebApiCustomers.Profiles
{ public class CustomerProfile : Profile { public CustomerProfile() { CreateMap<Customer, CustomerReadDto>(); CreateMap<CustomerCreateDto, Customer>(). ForMember(m => m.CreatedAt, o => o.MapFrom(s => DateTime.Now.Date)); } }
}

On the second line, CreateMap specifies a default value for the CreatedAt column, because we are taking de date from the system, not from the user.

Now we go back to the controller and inject two objects ICustomerRepository and IMapper, you constructor class should look like this:

private readonly ICustomerRepository _customerRepository;
private readonly IMapper _mapper; public CustomersController(ICustomerRepository customerRepository,IMapper mapper)
{ _customerRepository = customerRepository; _mapper = mapper;
}

Creating HTTP methods for CRUD Operations

Now are need to defined each HTTP method defined on the previous table

Get /api/customers

Here we are to define the action for a get method /api/customers and we have to return array of customers like this:

[HttpGet]
public async Task<IActionResult> GetAll()
{ var listCustomers = await _customerRepository.GetAllAsync(); var result = _mapper.Map<List<CustomerReadDto>>(listCustomers); return Ok(result); }

We have three lines of code :

List of customers :

var listCustomers = await _customerRepository.GetAllAsync();

Mapping list of Customers to CustomerReadDto :

var result = _mapper.Map<List<CustomerReadDto>>(listCustomers);

Return Response:

Get /api/customers/{id}

Here we are to define the action for a get method /api/customers/{id} and we have to return a CustomerReadDto object on, here we don't have to explain the following methods because are too similar :


[HttpGet]
public async Task<IActionResult> Get(int id)
{ var customerItem = await _customerRepository.GetAsync(id); var result = _mapper.Map<CustomerReadDto>(customerItem); return Ok(result);
}

POST /api/customers

Here we are to define the action for a POST method /api/customers like this;

[HttpPost]
public async Task<IActionResult> PostCustomer([FromBody]CustomerCreateDto customerCreateDto)
{ var customerToInsert = _mapper.Map<Customer>(customerCreateDto); await _customerRepository.AddAsync(customerToInsert); await _customerRepository.SaveAsync(); return CreatedAtAction("Get", new { id = customerToInsert.Id }, customerToInsert);
}

PUT /api/customers/{id}

Here we are to define the action for a PUT method /api/customers/{id} like this;

[HttpPut("{id}")]
public async Task<IActionResult> UpdateCustomer(int id, [FromBody] CustomerUpdateDto customerUpdateDto)
{ if (id != customerUpdateDto.Id) return BadRequest(); var customerToUpdate = _mapper.Map<Customer>.(customerUpdateDto); await _customerRepository.UpdateAsync(customerToUpdate); await _customerRepository.SaveAsync(); return NoContent();
}

Delete /api/customers/{id}

Here we are to define the action for a Delete method /api/customers/{id} like this;

[HttpDelete("{id}")]
public async Task<IActionResult> DeleteCustomer(int id)
{ await _customerRepository.DeleteAsync(id); await _customerRepository.SaveAsync(); return NoContent();
}

Swagger

This tool enables development across the entire API lifecycle, from design and documentation to testing and deployment.

Swagger is integrated to the project in a easy way in our services by default is enabled in our project.

If you defined the project following all steps, you could use the swagger page to test all your HTTP methods:

Conclusion

We learned about these topics.

  • Dependency Injection
  • Repository Pattern
  • AutoMapper
  • Create a web API project
  • Create Generic Repository for CRUD Operations

Source code WebApiCustomers

If you enjoyed this article, please subscribe and follow this series about Building clean web API Net Core 6.

Once suspended, learnwithandres will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, learnwithandres will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by learnwithandres will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If learnwithandres is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag learnwithandres:

learnwithandres consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging learnwithandres will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 在上下文侧边栏中设置文档智能组件 | Set up Document Intelligence Component in Contextual Sidebar
item.description:
随着 ServiceNow 的东京发布，我们对记录页面进行了一些很好的改进。 我在这里假设您已经安装了 Document Intelligence 组件。

首先，我们需要打开 UI Builder (UIB) 并导航到 Record 页面。 然后让我们转到我们的标签侧边栏组件：

并添加一个新标签。

我正在选择“从空容器开始”。

然后创建一个选项卡并选择一个图标。

在那个新创建的选项卡中，我现在可以选择一个组件。

现在我们需要做一些接线。 我创建了一个 GraphQL 数据代理来获取正确的 DI 任务 (di_task)。 由于我们在 di_task 表上有一个 source_table 和 source_record 字段，我们可以使用它来获取我们的 di_task。

我的 GraphQL 数据代理的属性是一个过滤器，因此我可以在 UIB 中设置正确的过滤器。

 [{ " name " : " filter " , " label " : " Filter for getting the right docIntel Task " , " description " : " Query conditions in the 'encoded query' format, for filtering the di_task table " , " readOnly " : false , " fieldType " : " condition_string " , " mandatory " : false , " defaultValue " : "" , " typeMetadata " : { " table " : " di_task " } }]

然后是一个简单的查询：

 query ( $filter : String ){ GlideRecord_Query { di_task ( queryConditions : $filter ) { _results { display_name { value }, sys_id { value } } } } }

现在让我们在 UIB 上连接过滤器并进行数据绑定。 我们也可以在过滤条件中使用记录页的props数据。

最后一点是对组件进行数据绑定。

就是这样，玩得开心！

一旦暂停，23thom 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，23thom 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，23thom 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 23thom 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 23thom 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 23thom 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

!This post was made for the ServiceNow Tokyo Release!

With the Tokyo Release of ServiceNow we got some nice improvements to the Record page. I assume here, that you installed the Document Intelligence component already.

First we need to open UI Builder (UIB) and navigate to the Record page. Then lets go to our tab sidebar component:

and add a new tab.

I'm selecting the "start from an empty container".

Then creating a tab and selecting an icon.

In that newly created tab I can now select a component.

Now we need to do some wiring. I created a GraphQL Data Broker to fetch me the the right DI task (di_task). As we have a source_table and source_record field on the di_task table, we can use that to get our di_task.

The Properties of my GraphQL data broker is a filter, so I can set the right filter in the UIB.

[{ "name": "filter", "label": "Filter for getting the right docIntel Task", "description": "Query conditions in the 'encoded query' format, for filtering the di_task table", "readOnly": false, "fieldType": "condition_string", "mandatory": false, "defaultValue": "", "typeMetadata": {"table": "di_task"} }]

And then a simple query:

query ($filter: String){ GlideRecord_Query { di_task(queryConditions: $filter) { _results { display_name { value }, sys_id{ value } } } }
}

Lets now wire up the Filter on the UIB and do the data binding. We can also use the props data of the record page in the filter conditions.

And the last point is to do the data binding on the component.

Thats it, have fun!

🌚 Enable dark mode🔠 Change your default font

📚 Adjust your experience level to see more relevant content

Once suspended, 23thom will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, 23thom will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by 23thom will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.
